items.title: ECO 348K Causal Inference
items.source_text: The course introduces how economists infer causality from data, covering the potential outcomes causal model, randomized controlled trials, regression discontinuity design, regression kink design, instrumental variables, and difference-in-differences. Additional topics include directed acyclic graphs, selection bias, bunching analysis, and machine learning applications to causal inference.

items.title: CRP 386 Urban GIS
items.source_text: Applied GIS course for planners covering spatial analysis, spatial statistics, 3D modeling of urban environments, GPS data integration, and cartographic representation for planning communication. Students develop skills in urban data visualization, site analysis, and geodatabase management using industry GIS platforms.

items.title: ECO 370M Behavioral Economics
items.source_text: Covers heuristics and biases, prospect theory, and departures from expected utility in choice under uncertainty. Topics include intertemporal choice with hyperbolic discounting, behavioral finance applications, nudge theory and libertarian paternalism, and the role of social norms in shaping economic behavior.

items.title: PBH 354 Introduction to Epidemiology
items.source_text: Introduction to epidemiologic methods covering descriptive epidemiology, disease surveillance systems, measures of association and causation, cohort and case-control study design, confounding and bias, and evidence-based decision analysis. Includes outbreak investigation methods and the application of epidemiologic evidence to public health policy.

items.title: GRG 333K Climate Change
items.source_text: Investigates climatic system changes across time scales and their effects on physical and ecological systems, examining past, present, and future climate conditions. Covers paleoclimatology, greenhouse gas forcing, climate projections, physical and ecological impacts of warming, climate vulnerability assessment, mitigation strategies, and climate policy frameworks.

items.title: GRG 470C Advanced Geographic Information Systems
items.source_text: Study of methods of spatial analysis, design and implementation of a geographic information system, vector and raster modeling, and advanced applications. Covers spatial analysis methods, network analysis, spatial interpolation, geostatistics, geospatial database design, and advanced cartographic visualization for research and professional applications.
